title: Emergent prethermal Bethe integrability in a periodically driven Rydberg chain
authors: Saptadip Roy, Arnab Sen, Diptiman Sen, K. Sengupta
abstract: We study a chain of periodically driven Rydberg atoms and identify a class of drive protocols for which the system exhibits emergent prethermal Bethe integrability at special drive frequencies. We provide a perturbative analytic expression of its Floquet Hamiltonian in the large drive amplitude regime. We demonstrate integrability of the leading term of this Floquet Hamiltonian at special drive frequencies, which we identify, by mapping it to the Hamiltonian of the paradigmatic spin-$1/2$ ${\rm XXZ}$ chain. We support our analytical results by exact diagonalization studies on finite chains. Our numerical results on level statistics, half-chain entanglement entropy, and longitudinal magnetization of the driven chain brings out its emergent integrable nature at the special drive frequencies which persists up to a large prethermal timescale.
summary: This paper explores how periodic driving can induce emergent integrability in a Rydberg atom chain. By using specific square-pulse driving protocols, the authors show that the system's effective Hamiltonian can be mapped to the integrable XXZ spin chain at certain frequencies. This leads to a prethermal regime where the system avoids thermalization and maintains conserved charges for extremely long periods. The findings are supported by both analytical mapping and numerical simulations of level statistics and entanglement.
main_problem: Investigating the emergence of prethermal Bethe integrability in periodically driven Rydberg atom chains and identifying drive protocols that prevent rapid thermalization.
main_result: The authors identify specific drive protocols and frequencies where the Floquet Hamiltonian maps to an integrable XXZ spin chain, leading to an extensive number of approximately conserved charges and suppressed heating for exponentially long timescales.
method: The study uses Floquet perturbation theory to derive effective Hamiltonians and exact diagonalization to analyze spectral statistics, entanglement entropy, and magnetization dynamics.
model_or_system: A one-dimensional chain of Rydberg atoms in the strong interaction limit, described by a PXP-like Hamiltonian with a Rydberg blockade constraint, subject to periodic driving (two-tone and asymmetric square pulse protocols).
key_observables: Level spacing ratio (r), half-chain entanglement entropy, longitudinal magnetization, and spectral form factor.
important_parameters: Drive frequency (omega), drive amplitude (lambda_0), phase accumulated during the drive (gamma), and the large drive amplitude regime (lambda_0 >> w_0, w_1).
main_assumptions: The analysis primarily focuses on the large drive amplitude regime and assumes the validity of the Floquet expansion for high-frequency driving.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the drive protocols; Figure 2 illustrates the mapping between the constrained Rydberg chain and the unconstrained XXZ model; Figure 3 compares spectral form factors at different frequencies.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the driven Rydberg system, presents the analytical derivation of the effective Hamiltonian using Floquet perturbation theory, establishes the mapping to the XXZ model, and provides numerical verification through exact diagonalization of spectral and dynamical observables.
why_interesting: It demonstrates a method to engineer integrability in non-integrable, constrained many-body systems via Floquet engineering, providing a way to control thermalization and preserve quantum information in Rydberg arrays.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 2. Schematic representation of the mapping between Fock states in the constrained Hilbert space of H(2) F (denoted by |ϕ⟩) and those of HXXZ (denoted by |ϕ′⟩). The mapping involves deleting a down-spin (↓) marked by red squares on the left of any up-spin (↑) of the PXP chain. (a) A state |ϕ⟩ with periodicity R = 3 and a number of up-spins in a block m1 = 1 is mapped to |ϕ′⟩with R′ = 2 and the same m1. (b) Example of a flippable up-spin in |ϕ⟩; this gets mapped to a ↑↓pair in |ϕ′⟩. (c) Example of a non-flippable up-spin in |ϕ⟩ which gets mapped to consecutive up-spins in |ϕ′⟩. See text for details.

FIG. 1. (a) Schematic representation of the two-tone square pulse drive protocol with λ(t) = +(−)λ0 for t ≤(>)T1/2 and w(t) = w0 ± w1 with time-period T1/3 as shown. (b) Schematic representation of the single-tone asymmetric square pulse (p ̸= 1/2) drive protocol where λ(t) = −(+)λ0 for t ≤(>)pT1.

FIG. 4. (a) Plot of M z(nT1) as a function of n starting from |vac⟩for γ/π = 2 (black) and 1.9 (blue), and similar plots starting from an |AFM⟩for γ/π = 2 (red) and 1.9 (green). The purple dotted line indicates the ITE average value of M z. For all plots λ0/w1 = 20 and L = 28. (b) Plot of M z st starting from |vac⟩as a function of λ0/w1 for L = 24 (red), 26 (blue) and 28 (green) at γ/π = 1. The plots show a gradual crossover to the thermal phase where M z st →−0.45 for λ0/w1 ≤1.0. The inset shows a plot of w1τ ∗/ℏ(where τ ∗= n∗T1) as a function of λ0/w1 for a system size L = 26, indicating exponential growth of n∗, the minimum number of drive cycles after which M z changes by 5% of M z(0) =...

FIG. 3. (a) Plot of r as a function of γ/π for w0/λ0 = 0.05, L = 26, and w1/λ0 = 0.05 (blue) and 0.1 (red), showing sharp dips at γ/π = m for integer m (ω1 = ω∗ m). The magenta (black) dashed lines r = 0.39 (0.527) correspond to values of r for the Poisson ensemble (COE). The insets show P(rp) dis- playing Poisson (Wigner-Dyson) statistics at γ = 2 (1.99)π. (b) Same as in (a) with w1/λ0 = 0.05 but around γ/π = 2 and for different L. The inset shows a closeup of the sharp rise of r from 0.39 to 0.527. (c) Plot of SL/2 as a function of EF T1/ℏ for Floquet quasienergies for γ/π = 2, L = 26, w0/λ0 = 0.05 and w1/λ0 = 0.05, displaying a wide spread. (d) Same as in (c) but for γ/π = 1.99 showing a...

FIG. 5. Plot of Frobenius norm N of commutator [HF , CPXP 3 ] as a function of γ/π, where the Floquet Hamiltonian HF is obtained numerically taking matrix logarithm of time evolu- tion operator U(T1, 0), in K = 0 sector of system sizes L = 20 (green), L = 22 (red) and L = 24 (blue). The inset shows behavior of N around γ = 2π. For all plots, w0/w1 = 1, q = 3 and λ0/w1 = 20. See text for details.

FIG. 1. Plot of half-chain entanglement SL/2 as a function of EF T1/ℏfor Floquet quasienergies for x = λ0T1/(πℏ) = 10, (a) px = 2, (b) px = 1.5, (c) px = 1.3, and (d) px = 1. For all plots the system size is L = 26 with PBC, and we have considered the translation symmetric and parity symmetric sector.

FIG. 2. (a) Plot of r as a function of px where x = λ0T1/(πℏ) = 10 for L = 20, R = +1 sector with OBC (red) and L = 26, K = 0, P = +1 sector with PBC showing sharp dips at px = m for integer m which correspond to special points. The magenta (black) dashed lines r = 0.39 (0.527) correspond to the value of r for the Poisson ensemble (COE). (b) Same as in (a) for K = 0, P = +1 sector with PBC but around px = 1, for different chain lengths L. The inset shows a closeup of a sharp rise of r from 0.39 to 0.527.

FIG. 3. (a) Plot of K(nT1) as a function of the number of drive cycles n for L = 24 (red), L = 26 (orange) and L = 28 (blue); the driven chain is assumed to have PBC and we are in the K = 0, P = +1 sector. For all plots w0/w1 = 1 and γ/π = 2. (b) same as in (a) but with γ/π = 1.67. (c) Plot of K(t) := 1 D2sec PDsec j1,j2=1 ei(Ej1 −Ej2 )t/ℏfor the eigenspectrum {Ej} of an undriven XXZ Hamiltonian in a longitudinal magnetic field with parameters ∆/J = −1/2, B/J = −3. The parameters are chosen so that H(2) F can be directly mapped to such an XXZ chain at γ = 2π. For all plots, Dsec is the Hilbert space dimension of the chosen magnetization sector, the chain lengths are chosen to be L = 18...

FIG. 4. (a) Plot of M x(nT1) as a function of the number of drive cycles n for γ/π = 2 (blue), 1.99 (green) and 1.9 (red) for |ψ(0)⟩= |vac⟩; the driven chain is assumed to have PBC and we are in the K = 0, P = +1 sector. For all plots w0/w1 = 1 and L = 28. (b) same as in (a) but for |ψ(0)⟩= |AFM⟩. The dotted lines show the corresponding ETH predicted DE values while the black dashed line indicated the ITE value.
